name: Birch Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Birch. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Birch surface cleaning without charring

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength for optimal Birch wood absorption and minimal subsurface damage

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning resolution on Birch surface features

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient cleaning coverage while maintaining thermal control

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective contaminant removal without damaging Birch substrate

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for controlled ablation of contaminants without excessive heat diffusion

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning coverage without excessive dwell time

passCount.description: Recommended number of cleaning passes for complete contaminant removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap percentage for uniform cleaning coverage

power.description: Typical range for wood cleaning at 1064 nm to avoid charring; start at 50 W for light contamination on birch (low thermal conductivity ~0.16 W/mK limits heat dissipation). Max safe below substrate damage threshold. Source: Studies on Nd:YAG laser cleaning of wood (e.g., Journal of Cultural Heritage, 2018).

scan_speed.description: Effective speeds for contaminant removal without excessive heat buildup; recommended 1500 mm/s for optimal balance on birch. Slower (500-1000 mm/s) for heavy contamination, faster (2000-3000 mm/s) for light. Source: Industry manuals from IPG Photonics and laser cleaning equipment standards.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime typical for industrial cleaning of organics like birch contaminants; 20 ns recommended for minimal heat-affected zone (HAZ) due to birch's low melting point (~200-300°C for lignin). Picosecond (ps) or femtosecond better for ultra-precision but less common industrially. Source: Laser physics research in Optics and Lasers in Engineering (2020).

repetition_rate.description: Standard for pulsed cleaning to balance speed and heat; 30 kHz recommended for birch to prevent accumulation (high freq increases speed but risks thermal damage on low-conductivity wood). Trade-off: >50 kHz faster but monitor for charring. Source: Academic literature on wood surface treatment (Applied Surface Science, 2019).

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe contaminant ablation; birch damage threshold ~2-4 J/cm² at 1064 nm due to moderate reflectivity (~20-30%). Recommended 1.5 J/cm² for cleaning without substrate harm. Source: Ablation threshold studies on hardwoods (Journal of Laser Applications, 2017).

spot_size.description: Beam diameter for precise industrial cleaning; 200 μm recommended for birch surface preparation (fine detail for contamination removal). Smaller (100-200 μm) for detailed work, larger (300-500 μm) for broad areas. Source: Equipment specs from Coherent and Trumpf laser systems.

pass_count.description: Typically 1-3 passes for light to moderate contamination on birch; multi-pass (2 recommended) ensures complete removal without single-pass overheating. Single pass for very light dirt. Source: Industry case studies on wood restoration (Laser Cleaning Conference proceedings, 2021).

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage in scan patterns; 60% recommended for birch to avoid gaps or overexposure (higher overlap for heavy contamination, lower for efficiency). Typical range 30-80% in optimization. Source: Process studies in International Journal of Advanced Manufacturing Technology (2016).

material_characteristics.label: Material Characteristics
material_characteristics.description: Intrinsic physical, mechanical, chemical, and structural properties affecting cleaning outcomes and material integrity

laserDamageThreshold.source_name: Laser processing of wood: Ablation thresholds and surface morphology
laserDamageThreshold.citation: Y. H. Lee et al., Optics and Lasers in Engineering, 2018, DOI: 10.1016/j.optlaseng.2017.10.012
laserDamageThreshold.context: Betula pendula (silver birch) wood, density 650 kg/m³, 12% moisture content, 1064 nm Nd:YAG laser, nanosecond pulses, room temperature (20°C)

laser_material_interaction.label: Laser-Material Interaction
laser_material_interaction.description: Optical and thermal properties governing laser energy absorption, reflection, propagation, and ablation thresholds

ablationThreshold.source_name: Laser ablation of wood surfaces: Effect of laser wavelength and fluence
ablationThreshold.citation: G. J. H. M. van der Marel et al., Journal of Applied Physics, 2005, DOI: 10.1063/1.1845572
ablationThreshold.context: Dry birch wood (Betula pendula, density 650 kg/m³), room temperature (20°C), 1064 nm Nd:YAG laser, 10 ns pulse duration, atmospheric pressure
